title: Quadlet container which sets Pod causes unwanted Pod dependency on untemplated container
body:
### Issue Description

If you have an `example@.container` which includes `Pod=example.pod` (note just a basic Pod, not a template Pod) then the `example-pod.service` ends up with a dependency on `example@.service`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create `example.pod`, just a basic pod, nothing special
2. Create `example@.container` with `Pod=example.pod` 
3. `systemd daemon-reload`


Note that there is no reference to `example@.container` in `example.pod`.


### Describe the results you received

`systemd show example-pod.service` includes:

```
Wants=example@example-pod.service
Before=example@example-pod.service
```

despite `example@example-pod.service` not being mentioned anywhere.

`systemctl edit example-pod.service` makes it clearer that `example-pod.service` has `Wants=example@.service` and `Before=example@.service`, which then get expanded to use `%p` for the template argument.

### Describe the results you expected

The pod should not depend on the template by default, any dependency like that should have to be explicitly configured. In my case, the template parameter is not meant to be a pod, so this is causing an extra unintended service to exist.

### podman info output

comments.body:
This also breaks `podman quadlet list`:

```
$ podman quadlet list
Error: querying systemd for unit status: Unit name example@.service is missing the instance name.
```

First, regarding:
```
Note that there is no reference to example@.container in example.pod.
```
When setting the `Pod` key in a `.container`, by default, a bidirectional link is established between the services created for the container and pod units. This is to make sure all containers belonging to a pod are started when the pod service is started.

Generally speaking when it comes to managing dependencies between templated Quadlet units, we have several different issues basically stating similar issues. For example see https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/17744#discussioncomment-9856920, https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24028, https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/25513.

As for you specific use case, I'm not sure I understand what behaviour you are expecting. If you want the `container` service to keep referencing the `pod` service, so that starting the former will also start the latter, but not vice-versa, you can add `StartWithPod=false` to your `.container` file. This will make the link between the service unidirectional.

@ygalblum Sorry for not being clear.

I understand the bidirectional link. The problem is, the Pod is adding a dependency on `example@.service`, i.e. without a template parameter. systemctl treats this as `example@pod-name-pod.service`, and that causes it to start a service I never asked for. I would expect there to only be a one-way link by default for template containers (i.e. example@foo.service depends on example-pod.service).

Essentially, I would expect Quadlets to not depend on templates by default, since it doesn't know what to put as the template parameter. Assuming the user always wants the pod's service name is odd IMO.

`StartWithPod=false` does indeed fix my issue, I was unaware of that option. Feel free to close this issue if that is the intended behaviour, but I do personally think it is surprising that a dependency on a template without a parameter is created by default.

> Feel free to close this issue if that is the intended behaviour, but I do personally think it is surprising that a dependency on a template without a parameter is created by default.

You are right. I agree that while the flag addresses the issue, Quadlet should have done that on its own. But, I wanted to first clarify what you expected to see, before going ahead and addressing it.

I'll open a PR to not add the dependency when its a template.
